core_concept: Nutrition: Food Groups, Nutrients, and How Food Fuels the Body
facts: Grains like bread and rice provide carbohydrates, which give the body energy to run, think, and play; without carbohydrates, you would feel tired.
Proteins from meat, eggs, and beans are used to build and repair muscles and other body tissues, not just for energy, so eating protein helps you grow strong.
Dairy foods such as milk and cheese contain calcium, a mineral that makes bones and teeth hard and strong; without enough calcium, bones can become weak.
Fruits and vegetables give your body vitamins to help it use other nutrients and fiber to move food through your digestive system; eating a variety of them keeps you healthy.
misconceptions: Some students think that proteins and carbohydrates are the same because both come from food, but proteins are for building tissues while carbohydrates are for energy.

Students often believe that eating just one type of fruit or vegetable is enough to get all the vitamins and fiber needed, not understanding that different fruits and vegetables provide different amounts and types of nutrients.
